commonProblems.h2: Peugeot 1007 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Peugeot 1007's TU3 and TU5 petrol engines dominate UK replacement enquiries, with cylinder pressure issues and head gasket failures most common. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your 1007.

problems.h4: Peugeot 1007 Cylinder Pressure Fluctuation - Why 1.4L TU3 Engines Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 1007 Urban Move, Active, Entry (2005-2010, KFV/KFW/KFU TU3 engines)
problems.rootCause: Worn piston rings or valve stem seals allow compression loss, often triggered by infrequent oil changes or short-journey driving patterns.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic compression test, lambda/O2 sensor inspection, exhaust leak check
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if pressure loss is sensor-related; won't fix mechanical wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head refurb, new valve seals, gaskets, timing components
repairOptions.longevity: Extends life 20k-40k miles if block is sound; risky if cylinder bores are scored

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt TU3 unit with upgraded rings/seals, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution for cars worth £1,500+; eliminates repeat failure risk

problems.recommendation: On a Peugeot 1007 valued under £1,500, a full rebuilt engine at £450-£950 often represents better value than a £450+ top-end rebuild that may not address underlying bore wear. We recommend getting quotes for a complete rebuilt unit first.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt TU3 engine prices for your Peugeot 1007

problems.h4: Peugeot 1007 Head Gasket Failure - TU5JP4 1.6L Engine Weak Point
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 1007 Sport, S, SE (2005-2010, NFU TU5JP4 1.6L petrol)
problems.rootCause: Thermal cycling and coolant neglect cause gasket degradation between cylinders; overheating from thermostat failure accelerates the process.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New thermostat, water pump, coolant flush, pressure test
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative only; won't fix an already-blown gasket

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head removal, skim, new gasket set, timing belt replacement
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block is undamaged; risk of warped head on high-mileage units

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt NFU engine with multi-layer steel gasket, upgraded components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term solution; eliminates repeat failure risk on valuable 1.6L models

problems.recommendation: If your 1.6L 1007 shows white smoke or coolant loss, budget for a full rebuilt NFU engine. Head gasket repairs on high-mileage TU5 units often uncover further damage, making replacement the more predictable investment.
problems.cta: Get quotes for NFU Peugeot 1007 engine replacement

problems.h4: Peugeot 1007 Timing Belt Failure - Interference Engine Catastrophe Risk
problems.affectedModels: All Peugeot 1007 petrol variants (KFV, KFW, KFU, NFU engines, 2005-2010)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 60,000-72,000 miles if not replaced per schedule
problems.rootCause: TU/ET engines are interference design; a snapped belt causes piston-to-valve contact, bending valves and potentially destroying the cylinder head.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New timing belt, tensioner, idler, water pump, labour
repairOptions.longevity: Essential maintenance; prevents catastrophic failure if done on time

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine after belt snap damage, upgraded components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only option after interference damage; repair is rarely economical

problems.recommendation: Never gamble with TU/ET timing belts. If your 1007 is due for a belt change, do it now. If the belt has snapped, a rebuilt engine is almost always more cost-effective than attempting a cylinder head repair on an interference-damaged block.
problems.cta: Compare Peugeot 1007 timing belt replacement vs engine rebuild costs

problems.h4: Peugeot 1007 Exhaust Leak Symptoms - False Lambda Sensor Codes
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 1007 Allure, Roland Garros, late-model Active (KFU ET3J4, 2007-2010)
problems.rootCause: Exhaust manifold gasket or downpipe joint leaks allow unmetered air into the exhaust stream, confusing lambda sensors and triggering rough idle or check engine light.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New manifold gasket, downpipe seal, lambda sensor check if needed
repairOptions.longevity: Highly effective if leak is the sole cause; quick turnaround

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New O2 sensor(s) plus exhaust seal inspection
repairOptions.longevity: Only address sensors after confirming exhaust integrity

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - If Leak Caused Secondary Damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt engine if prolonged lean running damaged valves or pistons
repairOptions.longevity: Rare outcome; most leaks are fixed at the exhaust stage

problems.recommendation: Start with an exhaust leak diagnosis. If your 1007 has rough idle with exhaust codes, a £120-£280 seal repair often resolves the issue. Only consider engine replacement if diagnostics confirm internal damage from prolonged incorrect fueling.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 1007 exhaust repair or engine replacement

problems.h4: Peugeot 1007 HDi EGR Failure - Diesel Power Loss & Smoke
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 1007 Premium HDi, GT Line HDi (9HZ DV6TED4, 2007-2010)
problems.rootCause: Carbon buildup in the EGR valve restricts exhaust recirculation flow, causing poor idle, black smoke, and loss of power; short-journey driving accelerates clogging.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: EGR valve removal, carbon clean, or blanking plate fit (where legal)
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for power restoration; may trigger emissions test concerns

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New or refurbished EGR valve, gaskets, coding reset
repairOptions.longevity: Longer-term fix; recommended if valve is mechanically worn

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - If Turbo or DPF Damaged
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt DV6 engine if EGR failure caused turbo oil contamination or DPF blockage
repairOptions.longevity: Only if secondary damage confirmed; most EGR issues are resolved earlier

problems.recommendation: On a 1007 HDi used for short trips, budget for an EGR clean every 40k miles. If power loss persists after cleaning, a £280-£550 valve replacement is usually more economical than risking turbo damage that could necessitate a full engine rebuild.
problems.cta: Compare Peugeot 1007 HDi EGR repair vs engine replacement quotes

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Peugeot 1007
finalCta.paragraph: The Peugeot 1007's low residual value makes engine failure feel like a write-off, but EngineMarket connects you with UK specialists offering rebuilt engines from £425-£1,350 - far below main dealer pricing. Every unit includes upgraded components addressing known failure points and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare quotes from vetted suppliers, choose supply-only or nationwide supply & fit, and keep your 1007 on the road without the dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Peugeot 1007 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Peugeot 1007 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.description: The 1.4L TU3 family powered most Peugeot 1007 models from launch, offering adequate performance for urban use with low running costs; three code variants (KFV, KFW, KFU) appeared across the range with minor ECU and injection differences.

items.title: Peugeot 1007 1.6L Petrol Engines

items.title: Peugeot 1007 1.6L Diesel Engines

engineYears.h2: Peugeot 1007 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Peugeot 1007's 2005-2010 production run, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original 1007 launched with 1.4L TU3 petrol engines only, establishing the model's urban-focused character and budget replacement profile.
keyChanges: Peugeot 1007 introduced with electric sliding doors as standard
1.4L TU3 petrol engines (KFV, KFW) only at launch
Urban Move, Active, Entry and Sport trims available
knownFor: Distinctive sliding doors define the model's identity
TU3 engines reliable but prone to sensor-related rough idle
Low residual values make affordable engine replacement critical
years.cta: Get quotes for 2005-2007 Peugeot 1007 replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 1007 1.4 engine replacement • Peugeot 1007 KFV engine • Peugeot 1007 rough idle fix

years.preview: Facelift Generation: HDi Diesel & ET3J4 Arrive
years.description: The 2007 facelift added the 1.6L HDi diesel and updated 1.4L ET3J4 petrol, broadening the 1007's appeal but introducing new failure patterns.
keyChanges: Facelift styling with revised front end and interior trim
1.6L HDi diesel (9HZ) introduced for Premium and GT Line
1.4L ET3J4 (KFU) replaced earlier TU3JP in some trims
mainEngines: 9HZ - 1.6L diesel DV6TED4 (Premium HDi, GT Line HDi)
knownFor: HDi engines deliver strong torque but suffer EGR/DPF issues on short trips
KFU ET3J4 shows exhaust leak symptoms triggering lambda codes
Facelift models command slightly higher used prices, justifying rebuilt engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 1007 HDi engine • Peugeot 1007 KFU exhaust leak • Peugeot 1007 1.6 petrol replacement

faq.h2: Peugeot 1007 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Peugeot 1007 engine in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Peugeot 1007 engines typically cost £425-£1,100 supply-only, depending on engine code and condition. Supply & fit adds £350-£650 labour. Used units start around £350 but carry shorter warranties.
keyPoints: 1.4L petrol rebuilt: £425-£950 supply-only
1.6L petrol rebuilt: £550-£1,100 supply-only
1.6L HDi diesel rebuilt: £650-£1,350 supply-only
Labour for supply & fit: £350-£650 typical
All rebuilt units include minimum 12-month warranty
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 1007 engine replacement quotes today

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in a Peugeot 1007?
items.answer: Yes, if your 1007 is otherwise sound and valued over £1,000. Rebuilt engines from £425 make replacement economical versus scrapping, especially with warranty protection against repeat failure.
keyPoints: Typical 1007 values: £800-£2,000 depending on trim/mileage
Rebuilt engine cost often under 50% of car's value
Warranty protects your investment for 12+ months
Supply & fit available nationwide for hassle-free replacement
Cheaper than buying another used 1007 with unknown history
items.cta: Find out if your Peugeot 1007 is worth an engine replacement

items.question: What are the most common Peugeot 1007 engine problems?
items.answer: Cylinder pressure fluctuation on 1.4L TU3 engines, head gasket failure on 1.6L TU5JP4, and EGR clogging on 1.6L HDi diesels dominate owner reports. Exhaust leaks causing false lambda codes are also frequent.
keyPoints: TU3 engines: pressure loss, rough idle, sensor errors
TU5JP4: head gasket stress, timing belt tensioner wear
HDi 9HZ: EGR valve clogging, turbo oil seal leaks
Exhaust manifold leaks mimic sensor failures
Timing belt critical on all - interference engine design
items.cta: Get quotes to fix common Peugeot 1007 engine issues

items.question: Which Peugeot 1007 engine is most reliable?
items.answer: The 1.4L TU3 (KFV/KFW) petrol engines are generally the most reliable when maintained, with simpler design and lower repair costs. Avoid neglected HDi diesels used only for short journeys.
keyPoints: KFV/KFW TU3: simple, affordable to repair, widely available
KFU ET3J4: improved emissions but similar reliability profile
NFU TU5JP4: more powerful but head gasket vulnerability
9HZ HDi: strong torque but EGR/DPF issues on urban use
Service history matters more than engine code alone

items.question: Can I fit a Peugeot 206 engine in my Peugeot 1007?
items.answer: Yes, many TU3 and TU5 engines are interchangeable between 1007 and 206/207 models, but ECU coding, wiring loom and ancillary differences require specialist adaptation.
keyPoints: KFV/KFW engines physically fit 206/207 donor cars
ECU immobiliser pairing essential for start-up
Ancillaries (alternator, AC) may need swapping
Professional fitment recommended for coding/immobiliser
Verify engine code matches before purchasing donor unit
items.cta: Check Peugeot 1007 engine compatibility with donor cars

items.question: How long does a Peugeot 1007 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engines dispatch in 1-3 working days. Supply & fit typically takes 1-2 days at a garage, plus delivery time. Total turnaround: 3-5 days from order to road-ready.
keyPoints: Engine dispatch: 1-3 working days typical
Garage fitment: 6-10 hours labour (1-2 days)
Post-fit testing and warranty validation included
Nationwide delivery available to your chosen garage
Expedited options may be available - ask at quote stage
items.cta: Get a timeline estimate for your Peugeot 1007 engine replacement

items.question: What warranty comes with a replacement Peugeot 1007 engine?
items.answer: All rebuilt, reconditioned and remanufactured engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Used engines typically carry 3-6 months.
keyPoints: Rebuilt/recon/reman: minimum 12-month unlimited mileage
Warranty covers parts and labour for engine-related failures
Used engines: typically 3-6 months, supplier-dependent
Always confirm exact terms with chosen supplier before ordering
Warranty valid UK-wide at any qualified garage
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 1007 engine warranty terms from UK suppliers

items.question: Where do I find my Peugeot 1007 engine code?
items.answer: The engine code is stamped on the engine block near the cylinder head, and also appears on your V5C logbook under "Engine number" or "Variant". Enter your reg above for instant DVLA verification.
keyPoints: Physical location: engine block, near cylinder head/sump
V5C logbook: check "Engine number" or "Variant" field
Service history/MOT certificates may list the code
Common codes: KFV, KFW, KFU (1.4L), NFU (1.6L), 9HZ (HDi)
DVLA lookup via registration is fastest and most reliable
items.cta: Identify your Peugeot 1007 engine code instantly

items.question: What causes Peugeot 1007 cylinder pressure fluctuation?
items.answer: Worn piston rings, valve stem seals or head gasket degradation allow compression loss, often triggered by infrequent oil changes, short-journey driving or missed timing belt service.
keyPoints: Primary causes: ring wear, valve seal hardening, gasket failure
Contributing factors: poor maintenance, short trips, overheating
Symptoms: rough idle, power loss, increased oil consumption
Diagnosis: compression test, leak-down test, borescope inspection
Prevention: regular oil changes, timely timing belt replacement
items.cta: Get quotes to address Peugeot 1007 compression issues

items.question: Should I repair or replace my Peugeot 1007 engine?
items.answer: If diagnostics confirm major internal wear (low compression, bearing knock), replacement is usually more economical. For minor issues (sensor errors, exhaust leaks), targeted repair may suffice.
keyPoints: Replacement recommended for: low compression, bearing noise, head gasket failure
Repair may suffice for: sensor errors, exhaust leaks, minor oil leaks
Consider car value: rebuild cost should be under 50% of vehicle value
Warranty coverage: rebuilt engines offer longer protection than repairs
Get both repair and replacement quotes before deciding
items.cta: Compare repair vs replacement quotes for your Peugeot 1007
